author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John x. 14, ‘I am the good shepherd,’ etc. Against the Donatists.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John x. 14, ‘I am the good shepherd,’ etc. Against the Donatists.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard the Lord Jesus setting forth to us the office of a good shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And herein He hath doubtless given us to know, as we may understand it, that there are good shepherds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet that the multitude of shepherds might not be understood in a wrong sense; He saith, “I am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And wherein He is the good Shepherd, He showeth in the words following; “The good Shepherd,” saith He, “layeth down His life for the sheep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, seeth the wolf coming, and fleeth; because he careth not for the sheep, for he is an hireling.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ then is the good Shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What was Peter? was he not a good shepherd?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did not he too lay down his life for the sheep?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What was Paul? what the rest of the Apostles? what the blessed Bishops, Martyrs, who followed close upon their times?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What again our holy Cyprian?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Were they not all good shepherds, not hirelings, of whom it is said, “Verily I say unto you, they have received their reward”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All these then were good shepherds, not simply for that they shed their blood, but that they shed it for the sheep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For not in pride, but in charity they shed it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For even among the heretics, they who for their iniquities and errors have suffered any trouble, vaunt themselves in the name of martyrdom, that with this fair covering disguised they may plunder the more easily, for wolves they are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if ye would know in what rank they are to be held, hear that good shepherd, the Apostle Paul, that not all who even give up their bodies in suffering to the flames, are to be accounted to have shed their blood for the sheep, but rather against the sheep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If,” saith he, “I speak with the tongues of men, and angels, but have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I should know all mysteries, and have all prophecy, and all faith, so that I could remove mountains, but have not charity, I am nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now a great thing truly is this faith that removes mountains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are indeed all great things; but if I have them without charity, saith he, not they, but I am nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But up to this point he hath not touched them, who glory in sufferings under the false name of martyrdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear how he toucheth, yea rather pierceth them through and through.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I should distribute,” saith he, “all my goods to the poor, and deliver my body to be burned.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now here they are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But mark what follows; “but have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, they have come to suffering, come even to the shedding of blood, yea come to the burning of the body; and yet it profiteth them nothing, because charity is lacking.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Add charity, they all profit; take charity away, all the rest profit nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What a good is this charity, Brethren!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more precious? what yieldeth greater light? or strength? or profit? or security?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many are the gifts of God, which even the wicked have, who shall say, “Lord, we have prophesied in Thy Name, in Thy Name have cast out devils, in Thy Name done many mighty works.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He will not answer, “Ye have not done them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in the Presence of so great a Judge, they will not dare to lie or boast of things they have not done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But for that they had not charity, He answereth them all, “I know you not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now how can he have so much as the smallest charity, who when even convicted, loves not unity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was then as impressing on good shepherds this unity, that our Lord was unwilling to mention many shepherds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not, as I have said already, that Peter was not a good shepherd, and Paul, the rest of the Apostles, and the holy Bishops who were after them, and blessed Cyprian.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All these were good shepherds; and notwithstanding to good shepherds, He commended not good shepherds, but a good Shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” saith He, “am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us question the Lord with such little understanding as we have, and in most humble discourse hold converse with so great a Master.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What sayest Thou, O Lord, Thou good Shepherd?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Thou art the good Shepherd, who art also the good Lamb; at once Pastor and Pasturage, at once Lamb and Lion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What sayest Thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us give ear and aid us, that we may understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” saith He, “am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is Peter? is he either not a shepherd, or a bad one?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see, if he be not a shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lovest thou Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou saidst to Him Lord, “Lovest thou Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he answered, “I do love Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And Thou to him, “Feed My sheep.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou, Thou, Lord, by Thine Own questioning, by the strong assurance of Thine Own words, madest of the lover a shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is a shepherd then to whom Thou didst commit Thy sheep to be fed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou didst Thyself entrust them, he is a shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us now see whether he be not a good one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This we find by the very question, and his answer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou didst ask, whether he loved Thee; he answered, “I do love Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou sawest his heart, that he answered truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is he not then good, who loveth so great a Good?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence that answer drawn from his inmost heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore was this Peter, who had Thine eyes in his heart for witnesses, sad because Thou askedst him not once only, but a second and a third time, that by a threefold confession of love, he might efface the threefold sin of denial; wherefore, I say, being sad that he was asked repeatedly by Him who knew what He was asking, and had given what He heard; wherefore being sad, did he return such an answer, “Lord, Thou knowest all things, Thyself knowest that I love Thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! in making such a confession, such a profession rather, would he lie?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In truth then, he made answer of his love to Thee, and from his inmost heart he gave utterance to a lover’s words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now Thou hast said, “A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then he is both a shepherd, and a good shepherd; nothing it is true to the power and goodness of the Shepherd of shepherds; but nevertheless even he is both a shepherd, and a good one; and all other such are good shepherds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What means it then, that to good shepherds Thou dost set forth One Only Shepherd, but that in One Shepherd Thou teachest unity? and the Lord Himself explains this more clearly by my ministry, putting you, beloved, in remembrance by this Gospel, and saying, “Hear ye what I have set forth; I have said, ‘I am the good Shepherd;’ because all the rest, all the good shepherds, are My members.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One Head, One Body, One Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then both the Shepherd of shepherds, and the shepherds of the Shepherd, and the sheep with their shepherds under The Shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is all this, but what the Apostle says?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore if Christ be even so, with good reason doth Christ in Himself containing all good shepherds, set forth One, saying, “‘I am the good Shepherd.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘I am,’ I Alone am, all the rest with Me are one in unity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso feedeth without Me, feedeth against Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘He that gathereth not with Me, scattereth.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear then this unity more forcibly set forth; “Other sheep,” saith He, “I have which are not of this fold.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He was speaking to the first fold of the stock of the fleshly Israel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there were others of the stock of the faith of this Israel, and they were yet without, were among the Gentiles, predestinated, not yet gathered in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These He knew who had predestinated them; He knew, who had come to redeem them with the shedding of His Own Blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saw them who did not yet see Him; He knew them who yet believed not on Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Other sheep,” saith He, “I have which are not of this fold;” because they are not of the stock of the flesh of Israel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But nevertheless they shall not be outside of this fold, “for them also I must bring, that there may be One Fold, and One Shepherd.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With good reason then to This Shepherd of shepherds, doth His Beloved, His Spouse, His Fair One, but by Him made fair, before by sin deformed, beautiful afterward through pardon and grace, speak in her love and ardour after Him, and say to Him, “Where feedest Thou?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And observe how, by what transport this spiritual love is here animated.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And far better are they by this transport delighted, who have tasted ought of the sweetness of this love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They hear this properly, who love Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in them, and of them, doth the Church sing this in the Song of Songs; who love Christ, as it seemed without beauty, yet the Only Beautiful One.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For we saw Him,” it is said, “and He had neither beauty nor comeliness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such He appeared on the Cross, such when crowned with thorns did He exhibit Himself, disfigured, and without comeliness, as if He had lost His power, as if not the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such seemed He to the blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is in the person of the Jews that Isaiah said this, “We saw Him, and He had no beauty nor comeliness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When it was said, “If He be the Son of God, let Him come down from the Cross.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saved others, Himself He cannot save.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And smiting Him on the head with a reed, they said, “Prophesy unto us, thou Christ, who smote Thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because “He had neither beauty nor comeliness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As such did ye Jews see Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “blindness hath happened in part to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles enter in,” until the other sheep come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then blindness hath happened, therefore did ye see the Comely One without comeliness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For had ye known Him, ye would never have crucified the Lord of Glory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But ye did it, because ye knew Him not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet He who as though without beauty bare with you, all Beauteous as He was, prayed for you; “Father,” saith He, “forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He were without comeliness, how is it that she loveth Him, who saith, “Tell me, O Thou whom my soul loveth”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is it that she loveth Him? how is it that she burneth for Him? how is it that she feareth so much to stray from Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is it that she hath so great delight in Him, that her only punishment is to be without Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What would there be for which He should be loved, if He were not beautiful?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how could she love Him so, if He appeared to her as He did to those blind men persecuting Him, and knowing not what they do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As what then did she love Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As “comely in form above the sons of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Comely in form above the sons of men, grace is poured abroad in Thy Lips.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then from these Thy Lips, “Tell me, O Thou whom my soul loveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me,” says she, “O Thou whom,” not my flesh, but, “my soul loveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down in the midday; lest haply I light, as one veiled, upon the flocks of Thy companions.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It seems obscure, obscure it is; for it is a mystery of the sacred marriage bed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For she says, “The King hath brought me into His chamber.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of such a chamber is this a mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But ye who are not as profane kept off from this chamber, hear ye what ye are, and say with her, if with her ye love (and ye do love with her, if ye are in her); say all, and yet let one say, for unity saith; “Tell me, O Thou whom my soul loveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they had one soul to Godward, and one heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down in the midday?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does the midday signify?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Great heat, and great brightness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, “make known to me who are Thy wise ones,” fervent in spirit, and brilliant in doctrine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Make known to me Thy Right Hand, and men learned in heart, in wisdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To them may I cleave in Thy Body, to them be united, with them enjoy Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me then, “tell me, where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down in the midday;” lest I fall upon them who say other things of Thee, entertain other sentiments of Thee; believe other things of Thee, preach other things of Thee; and have their own flocks, and are Thy companions; for that they live of Thy table, and handle the sacraments of Thy table.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For companions are so called, because they eat together, messmates as it were.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such are reproved in the Psalm; “For if Mine enemy had spoken great things against Me, I would surely have hidden Myself from him; and if he that hated Me had spoken great things against Me, I would surely have hidden Myself from him; but thou a man of one mind with Me, My guide, and My familiar, who didst take sweet meats together with Me, in the house of God we walked with consent.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then now against the house of the Lord with dissent, but that “they have gone out from us, but they were not of us?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “O Thou whom my soul loveth,” that I may not fall upon such, Thy companions, but companions such as Samson’s were, who kept not faith with their friend, but wished to corrupt his wife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, that I may not fall upon such as these, “that I may not light upon them,” that is, fall upon them, “as one that is veiled,” as one that is concealed, that is, and obscure, not as established upon the mountain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Tell me” then, “O thou whom my soul loveth, where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down in the midday;” who are the wise and faithful in whom Thou dost specially rest, lest by chance as in blindness I fall upon the flocks, not Thy flocks, but the flocks of Thy companions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou didst not say to Peter, “Feed thy sheep,” but, “Feed My sheep.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then the “good Shepherd,” and, “the Comely in form above the sons of men,” make answer to this beloved one; make answer to her whom He hath made beautiful from among the children of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear ye what He answereth, and understand, beware of that wherewith He alarmeth, love that which He adviseth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then doth He answer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How free from soft caresses, yea, to her caresses He returneth severity!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is sharp that He may bind her closely, that He may keep her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If thou know not thyself,” saith He, “O thou fair one among women:” for however fair others may be by the gifts of thy Spouse, they are heresies, fair in outward ornament, not within: fair are they without, and outwardly they shine, they disguise themselves by the name of righteousness; “but all the beauty of the King’s daughter is within.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If” then “thou know not thyself;” that thou art one, that thou art throughout all nations, that thou art chaste, that thou oughtest not to corrupt thyself with the disordered converse of evil companions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If thou know not thyself,” that in uprightness, “he hath espoused thee to Me, to present you a chaste Virgin to Christ;” and that in uprightness thou shouldest present thine own self to Me, lest by evil converse, “as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds too should be corrupted from my purity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If,” I say, “thou know not thyself” to be such, “go thy way; go thy way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to others I shall say, “Enter thou into the joy of thy Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To thee I shall not say, “Enter in;” but, “Go thy way;” that thou mayest be among those, who “went out from us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go thy way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is, “if thou know not thyself,” then, “go thy way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou know thyself, enter in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, “if thou know not thyself, go thy way by the footsteps of the flocks, and feed thy kids in the tents of the shepherds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Go thy way by the footsteps,” not “of the Flock,” but, “of the flocks, and feed,” not as Peter, “My sheep,” but, “thy kids; in the tents,” not “of the Shepherd,” but, “of the shepherds;” not of unity, but of dissension; not established there, where there is One flock and One Shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The beloved one was confirmed, edified, made stronger, prepared to die for her Spouse and to live with her Spouse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These words which I have quoted out of the Holy Song of Songs, of a kind of bridal song of the Bridegroom and the Bride (for it is a spiritual wedding, wherein we must live in great purity, for Christ hath granted to the Church in spirit that which His Mother had in body, to be at once a Mother and a Virgin); these words, I say, the Donatists accommodate to their own perverted sense in a very different meaning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how I will not conceal from you, and what ye may answer them, I will, by the Lord’s help, as well as I shall be able, briefly recommend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When then we begin to press them with the light of the Church’s unity spread over the whole world, and demand of them to show us any testimony out of the Scriptures, where God hath foretold that the Church should be in Africa, as if all the rest of the nations were lost; they are in the habit of taking this testimony in their mouths, and saying; “Africa is under the midday sun; the Church then” they say, “asking the Lord where He feedeth, where He lieth down; He answereth, ‘Under the midday sun;’” as if the voice of her who put the question, were, “Tell me, O Thou whom my soul loveth, where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down;” and the Voice of Him who answereth, were, “Under the midday sun;” that is, in Africa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then it be the Church which asketh, and the Lord maketh answer where he feedeth, in Africa, because the Church was in Africa; then she who asketh was not in Africa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Tell me,” she saith, “O Thou whom my soul loveth, where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down;” and He maketh answer to some Church out of Africa, “Under the midday sun,” in Africa I lie down, in Africa I feed, as if it were, “I do not feed in thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I repeat, if she who asketh is the Church, which no one disputes, which not even themselves gainsay; and they hear something about Africa; then she who asketh is out of Africa; and because it is the Church, the Church is out of Africa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But see, I admit that Africa is under the midday sun; although Egypt is rather under the meridian, under the midday sun than Africa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now after what fashion This Shepherd is there in Egypt, they who know, will acknowledge; and for them that know not, let them enquire how large a flock lie gathereth there, how great a multitude He hath of holy men and women who utterly despise the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That flock hath so increased, that it hath expelled superstitions even thence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To pass over how it hath in its increase banished thence the whole superstition of idols, which had been firmly fixed there; I admit what you say, O evil companions; I admit it altogether, I agree that Africa is in the South, and that Africa is signified in that which is said, “Where feedest Thou, where dost Thou lie down under the midday sun?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do ye too equally observe how that up to this point these are the words of the Bride, and not yet of the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hitherto it is the Bride that saith, “Tell me, O Thou whom my soul loveth, where Thou feedest, where Thou dost lie down in the midday, lest by chance I light, as one veiled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O thou deaf, and blind one, if in the “midday” thou seest Africa, why in her that is “veiled” dost thou not see the Bride?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Tell me,” she said, “O Thou whom my soul loveth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Without doubt she addresses her Spouse, when she says, “whom” [in the masculine] “my soul loveth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just as if it were said, “Tell me, O thou whom [in the feminine] “my soul loveth;” we should understand that the Bridegroom spake these words to His Bride; so when you hear, “Tell me, O thou whom” (in the masculine) “my soul loveth, where Thou feedest, where Thou liest down;” add to this, to her words belongs also what follows, “In the midday.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am asking, “where Thou feedest in the midday, lest by chance I light as one veiled upon the flocks of Thy companions.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I consent entirely, I admit what you understand of Africa; it is signified by “the midday.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But then as you understand it, the Church of Christ beyond the sea is addressing her Spouse, in fear of falling into the African error, “O Thou whom my soul, loveth, tell me,” teach me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I hear that “in the midday,” that is in Africa, there are two parties, yea rather many schisms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Tell me,” then, “where Thou feedest,” what sheep belong to Thee, what fold Thou biddest me love there, whereunto ought I to unite myself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lest by chance I light as one veiled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they mock me as if I were concealed, they mock me as destroyed, as though I existed nowhere else.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lest,” then, “as one veiled,” as if concealed, “I light upon the flocks,” that is, upon the congregarious of the heretics, “thy companions; the Donatists, the Maximinianists, the Rogatists and all the other pests who gather without, and who therefore scatter; “Tell me,” I pray Thee, if I must seek my Shepherd there, that I fall not into the gulf of re-baptizing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I exhort you, I beseech you by the sanctity of such nuptials, love this Church, be ye in this holy Church, be ye this Church; love the good Shepherd, the Spouse so fair, who deceiveth no one, who desireth no one to perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pray too for the scattered sheep; that they too may come, that they too may acknowledge Him, that they too may love Him; that there may be One Flock and One Shepherd.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us turn to the Lord, etc.
